Test Case: PHASE 1 — USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING: Master Data + Setup
Tester Role: Manufacturing Manager / Planner
Enumerate all Workstation Types on the live site
Steps: Navigate to Workstation Type list, paste the full list of records into the Notes column
Expected Result: Tester records every Workstation Type name present. No expected count. The list becomes the source of truth for UT-1.04 and UT-1.05

Test Case: Verify product_line custom field on Workstation Type
Steps: Open any Workstation Type, check for product_line field
Expected Result: Select field visible with options: Computer Paper, Label, Carton, All

Test Case: Verify default_stage custom field on Workstation Type
Steps: Open Flexo Press Workstation Type, set default_stage
Expected Result: Link field to Production Stage is visible and selectable (e.g., Printing)

Test Case: Set product_line on any Computer Paper type
Steps: Pick any Workstation Type from UT-1.01 that runs Computer Paper work (e.g., Collator or Numbering), set product_line = Computer Paper, save
Expected Result: Saves successfully. Value persists on reload

Test Case: Set product_line on any Label type
Steps: Pick any Workstation Type from UT-1.01 that runs Label work, set product_line = Label, save
Expected Result: Saves successfully. Value persists on reload

Test Case: Verify custom fields on Workstation form
Steps: Open any Workstation document, scroll to custom section
Expected Result: Fields visible: product_line, max_width_mm, max_colors, max_speed_per_hour, location_note

Test Case: Create a new Workstation for Flexo Press #1
Steps: Create Workstation: name=Flexo Press #1, type=Flexo Press, product_line=Label, max_width_mm=450, max_colors=6, max_speed_per_hour=8000, location_note=Bay 1
Expected Result: Saves successfully. All custom fields persist on reload

Test Case: Create a Workstation for Corrugator #1
Steps: Create Workstation: type=Corrugator, product_line=Carton, max_width_mm=2500, max_speed_per_hour=150
Expected Result: Saves successfully

Test Case: Create a Workstation for Collator #1
Steps: Create Workstation: type=Collator, product_line=Computer Paper, max_speed_per_hour=5000
Expected Result: Saves successfully

Test Case: Verify max_colors accepts integer only
Steps: Open Flexo Press #1, enter 'abc' in max_colors field
Expected Result: Frappe rejects non-integer input or field only accepts numbers

Test Case: Verify Production Stage seed records include all factory flow stages
Steps: Navigate to Production Stage list
Expected Result: At least 20 records covering all product-line flows. Computer Paper: Design, Pre-Press, Printing, Numbering, Collating, Wrapping, Packing. Label: Design, Pre-Press, Printing, Die Cutting, Re-winding, Slitting, Finishing. Carton: Corrugating, Pasting, Creasing, Slotting, Creasing & Slotting (legacy), Stitching, Gluing, Folding & Gluing (legacy), Bundling, Packing

Test Case: Verify Printing stage fields
Steps: Open Printing stage
Expected Result: stage_name=Printing, product_line=All, sequence=10, is_qc_point=unchecked

Test Case: Verify Wrapping is a QC point
Steps: Open Wrapping stage
Expected Result: is_qc_point=checked, product_line=Computer Paper, sequence=40

Test Case: Verify Packing is a QC point
Steps: Open Packing stage
Expected Result: is_qc_point=checked, product_line=All, sequence=90

Test Case: Create a new Production Stage
Steps: Create: stage_name=Laminating, product_line=Label, sequence=25, is_qc_point=unchecked
Expected Result: Saves successfully. Appears in list between Die Cutting (20) and Finishing (30) when sorted by sequence

Test Case: Verify duplicate stage name rejected
Steps: Try creating another stage named Printing
Expected Result: Frappe rejects duplicate (naming rule = set by user on stage_name)

Test Case: Add workstation to a Production Stage
Steps: Open Printing stage, add Workstation Stage row: workstation=Flexo Press #1, is_preferred=checked, hourly_rate=8000, setup_time_mins=30
Expected Result: Row saves in child table. Flexo Press #1 appears as preferred machine for Printing

Test Case: Add multiple workstations to one stage
Steps: Open Printing, add Flexo Press #2 row: is_preferred=unchecked, hourly_rate=6000, setup_time_mins=45
Expected Result: Both rows visible. Only one marked preferred

Test Case: Verify hourly_rate fallback logic documented
Steps: Leave hourly_rate blank on a Workstation Stage row, save
Expected Result: Saves with blank hourly_rate (system should fall back to workstation.max_speed_per_hour at scheduling time)

Test Case: Verify 8 seed Waste Reasons exist
Steps: Navigate to Waste Reason list
Expected Result: Setup Waste, Misprinted, Off-Register, Torn Web, Material Defect, Wrong Die, Colour Mismatch, Trim Waste

Test Case: Verify category options
Steps: Open Setup Waste, check category dropdown
Expected Result: Options: Setup, Running, Material Defect, Operator Error, Other

Test Case: Disable a waste reason
Steps: Open Trim Waste, uncheck enabled, save
Expected Result: enabled=0. Record still exists but should not appear in Production Entry waste_reason dropdown (future Phase 2 validation)

Test Case: Create a new Waste Reason
Steps: Create: reason_name=Blade Wear, category=Running, enabled=checked
Expected Result: Saves successfully

Test Case: Verify 8 seed Downtime Reasons exist
Steps: Navigate to Downtime Reason list
Expected Result: Mechanical Breakdown, Electrical Fault, Power Outage, Material Shortage, Planned Maintenance, Die Changeover, Operator Unavailable, Waiting for QC

Test Case: Verify is_planned flag on Planned Maintenance
Steps: Open Planned Maintenance
Expected Result: is_planned=checked, category=Planned Maintenance

Test Case: Verify is_planned unchecked on Mechanical Breakdown
Steps: Open Mechanical Breakdown
Expected Result: is_planned=unchecked, category=Mechanical

Test Case: Create a new Downtime Reason
Steps: Create: reason_name=Ink Shortage, category=Material Shortage, is_planned=0
Expected Result: Saves successfully

Test Case: Verify production_status on Job Card Computer Paper
Steps: Open any submitted Job Card Computer Paper
Expected Result: production_status field visible in Production Tracking section, value=Not Started, field is read-only

Test Case: Verify production_status on Job Card Label
Steps: Open any submitted Job Card Label
Expected Result: production_status field visible, value=Not Started, read-only

Test Case: Verify production_status on Job Card Carton
Steps: Open any submitted Job Card Carton
Expected Result: production_status field visible, value=Not Started, read-only

Test Case: Verify current_stage on all job card types
Steps: Open one of each job card type
Expected Result: current_stage Link field visible, blank, read-only

Test Case: Confirm production_status cannot be manually edited
Steps: Try clicking production_status dropdown on a job card
Expected Result: Field is read-only. Cannot be changed by user

Test Case: Verify fixtures export correctly
Steps: Run: bench export-fixtures --app production_log
Expected Result: Custom Fields, Production Stage, Waste Reason, Downtime Reason, Workstation Type records all exported to fixtures/ folder

Test Case: Verify fixtures import on fresh site
Steps: Import fixtures on a clean bench site
Expected Result: All master data recreated correctly — no errors

Test Case: Manufacturing Manager can CRUD all new doctypes
Steps: Login as Manufacturing Manager role, try create/read/update/delete on Production Stage, Waste Reason, Downtime Reason
Expected Result: Full CRUD access on all three doctypes

Test Case: Manufacturing User can only read new doctypes
Steps: Login as Manufacturing User role, try to create a Production Stage
Expected Result: Read access works. Create/Write/Delete buttons not visible or action blocked

Test Case: Production Log User can only read new doctypes
Steps: Login as Production Log User role, navigate to Production Stage list
Expected Result: Read access only. No create/edit/delete
